data: H2 molecule, STO-3G, Parity + 2-qubit reduction, 2 qubits; 13 bond lengths (0.50-3.00 Å); same Hamiltonian as baseline
primary_metric_definition: |VQE_total - classical_exact_total| in mHartree; chemical accuracy threshold = 1.6 mHa
method: VQE with hardware-efficient ansatz (RY-RZ + CNOT) + COBYLA optimizer, cqlib StatevectorSimulator
ansatz: hardware-efficient (RY-RZ per qubit + CNOT chain), Hartree-Fock init on qubit 0
convergence: All 13 bond lengths converged in 1 iteration (1 ansatz layer sufficed); iterative layer escalation not triggered
limitations: Simulator-only results (cqlib StatevectorSimulator), NOT real quantum hardware. H2 is a 2-qubit problem where hardware-efficient ansatz is fully expressive, so VQE matches exact diagonalization to within 0.1 mHa — this favorable agreement does NOT generalize to larger molecules requiring more qubits. No shot noise (statevector, not sampling). Equilibrium bond length measured at 0.75 Å (closest tabulated key to 0.74 Å).
